title: The Lone Wolf
description: The Lone Wolf — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "The Lone Wolf" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: Table of Contents

memes.label: Antwerp
memes.description: The Belgian port city that serves as the communications hub of the international underworld, where coded telegrams coordinate criminal operations across Europe.

memes.label: Aviation
memes.description: The technology of powered flight — aeroplanes, pilots, and aerial combat — that enables Michael Lanyard's escape from France and symbolizes the dawn of a new era in The Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Bannon
memes.description: An American businessman travelling in Paris who serves as a guardian figure to Lucy Shannon — a reminder of the legitimate world that exists beyond Lanyard's criminal milieu.

memes.label: Belgian Underworld
memes.description: The Antwerp-based node of the international criminal network, serving as the communications hub through which the Pack coordinates its operations across Europe via coded telegrams.

memes.label: Bois de Boulogne
memes.description: The large public park on the western edge of Paris, site of the climactic car chase where De Morbihan's men pursue Michael Lanyard and De Morbihan's own car crashes.

memes.label: Bourke
memes.description: A quick, compact, dangerous little Irishman who was Michael Lanyard's earliest associate in Paris and a fixture at the restaurant Troyon's.

memes.label: Bourke's Death
memes.description: The death of Bourke — Michael Lanyard's earliest associate and closest friend — the event that serves as the emotional and narrative catalyst for Lanyard's return to Paris and his eventual reformation.

memes.label: British Secret Service
memes.description: The British government intelligence service that becomes peripherally involved in the Lone Wolf affair through the Huysman plans — secret military documents stolen alongside Madame Omber's jewels.

memes.label: Captain Vauquelin
memes.description: A French military aviator who pilots the aeroplane that carries Michael Lanyard across the English Channel — a figure of courage and professional skill who becomes an unwitting ally in Lanyard's escape.

memes.label: Channel Crossing
memes.description: The daring cross-Channel aeroplane flight by which Michael Lanyard escapes from France to England, piloting through fog and danger in one of the novel's most dramatic sequences.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: Index of all characters appearing in Louis Joseph Vance's The Lone Wolf (1914), sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Colophon
memes.description: Colophon and publishing information.

memes.label: Concepts
memes.description: Index of thematic concepts explored in The Lone Wolf (1914) — from honour among thieves to espionage and aviation.

memes.label: Criminal Code
memes.description: The unwritten code of professional ethics governing the behaviour of criminals — particularly gentleman thieves — as depicted in Louis Joseph Vance's 1914 novel The Lone Wolf, where honour among thieves is both a practical necessity and a moral framework.

memes.label: Daybreak Resolution
memes.description: The final resolution of the novel in Chapter XXVII ("Daybreak"), in which Wertheimer reveals his true identity as a Scotland Yard special agent — transforming the entire preceding narrative from a criminal pursuit into a secret operation.

memes.label: De Morbihan
memes.description: A French count and man-about-town with deep connections to the Parisian underworld, who attempts to recruit the Lone Wolf into a criminal syndicate.

memes.label: De Morbihan's Plot
memes.description: The elaborate plot orchestrated by Count De Morbihan to draw Michael Lanyard into the Pack's criminal enterprise — a scheme that begins with a seemingly innocent dinner invitation and ends with an ultimatum.

memes.label: Disguise
memes.description: The art of physical concealment and misdirection practised by the Lone Wolf to evade detection by police and criminal rivals.

memes.label: Double Life
memes.description: The central psychological condition of Michael Lanyard — the simultaneous existence as gentleman and thief, public man and secret criminal, which drives the novel's exploration of identity and authenticity.

memes.label: Ducroy
memes.description: Monsieur Hector Ducroy, the French Minister of War, to whom Lanyard delivers stolen military plans in a midnight visit.

memes.label: Ekstrom
memes.description: An agent of the international criminal underworld who applies pressure on the Lone Wolf through threats and manipulation.

memes.label: Espionage
memes.description: Espionage and intelligence operations in *The Lone Wolf* — the theft of the Huysman war plans, German spy rings in Paris, and the shadow war between nations that provides the novel's geopolitical backdrop.

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Chronological index of major plot events in The Lone Wolf (1914), from the opening burglary to the final resolution.

memes.label: Faubourg Saint-Germain Heist
memes.description: The meticulous midnight burglary of Madame Omber's Passy mansion by Michael Lanyard — a return to his cracking skills — in which he recovers the Omber jewels with Lucy Shannon as his lookout, as depicted in Chapters XV–XVI ("Sheer Impudence" and "Restitution").

memes.label: Flight from Paris
memes.description: The desperate flight of Michael Lanyard from Paris after the Pack's attempt on his life and the police pursuit, spanning a night of narrow escapes through the city's streets and culminating in his departure for London.

memes.label: Franco-German Espionage
memes.description: The international intelligence conflict between France and Germany that forms the political backdrop of The Lone Wolf — German agents sought the Huysman war plans while Scotland Yard and the Sûreté worked to recover them.

memes.label: French Police
memes.description: The French law enforcement apparatus that pursues the Lone Wolf alongside Scotland Yard, operating through both uniformed police and the detective bureau known as the Sûreté.

memes.label: Gare du Nord
memes.description: The major Paris railway station that serves as a critical transit point in the Lone Wolf's flight from his pursuers and a key location in the novel's climax.

memes.label: Gentleman Thief
memes.description: The code of conduct, professional ethics, and social camouflage practised by the gentleman thief — a criminal archetype embodied by Michael Lanyard in Louis Joseph Vance's 1914 novel The Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Greggs
memes.description: Mr. Greggs, Bannon's private secretary and right-hand man — a minor Pack operative whose arrest and suicide mark a turning point in the underworld's campaign against the Lone Wolf.

memes.label: High Society
memes.description: The world of Parisian high society that Michael Lanyard infiltrates and exploits — a realm of salons, gambling tables, and titled personages that forms the backdrop of his criminal career.

memes.label: Honour
memes.description: The gentleman's code of ethics that governs Michael Lanyard's conduct — a thief who steals property but not lives, and who holds loyalty above profit.

memes.label: Honour Among Thieves
memes.description: The unwritten principle that even criminals operate by codes of professional respect and mutual recognition — a concept tested and ultimately betrayed by the Pack in The Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Huysman Plans Delivery
memes.description: The midnight delivery of the stolen Huysman war plans to the French Minister of War, Monsieur Ducroy — an act of patriotic restitution by the Lone Wolf that blurs the line between criminal and patriot.

memes.label: Impasse Stanislas
memes.description: A dead-end street in Paris where Lanyard and Lucy Shannon take temporary refuge during their flight — a brief sanctuary that proves, like all sanctuaries in the novel, to be temporary.

memes.label: Impasse Stanislas Escape
memes.description: The escape of Michael Lanyard and Lucy Shannon from the Pack's surveillance at the Impasse Stanislas hideout, a tense nighttime flight through rain-swept Paris streets.

memes.label: Jewel Theft
memes.description: The Lone Wolf's criminal specialty — the stealthy acquisition of portable valuables from the homes of the wealthy elite in Paris and London.

memes.label: La Santé Prison
memes.description: The notorious Paris prison — La Santé — where members of the criminal underworld are sent after capture, referenced throughout The Lone Wolf as the fate awaiting those who fail.

memes.label: La Tour Pointue
memes.description: La Tour Pointue — Parisian slang for the Préfecture de Police headquarters on the Quai de Marché Neuf, the city's central police building and notorious jail.

memes.label: L'Abbaye Council
memes.description: "The Pack council at L'Abbaye where Michael Lanyard is formally introduced to the syndicate and given a choice — join or be destroyed."

memes.label: Lanyard's Confession
memes.description: The pivotal scene in which Michael Lanyard confesses his identity as the Lone Wolf to Lucy Shannon — a moment of emotional vulnerability that marks the beginning of his moral transformation.

memes.label: Lanyard's Return
memes.description: Michael Lanyard's return to Paris after years abroad, a decision that sets in motion the entire chain of events in the novel — his reunion with old associates, his rediscovery by the Pack, and the unravelling of his carefully protected identity.

memes.label: Locations
memes.description: Index of all places featured in The Lone Wolf (1914), from Parisian streets to European capitals.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The city to which Michael Lanyard retreats when Paris becomes too dangerous, and the home of Scotland Yard — the force that pursues him.

memes.label: Lucy's Disappearance
memes.description: The unexplained disappearance of Lucy Shannon — Lanyard's love interest — who vanishes from the garden of the Impasse Stanislas hideout, leaving Lanyard to search Paris for three desperate days.

memes.label: Lucy Shannon
memes.description: An American woman in Paris whose relationship with Michael Lanyard offers him a path to redemption from his life as the Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Madame Omber
memes.description: A wealthy Parisian aristocrat whose famous jewels become the target of both the Lone Wolf and the criminal syndicate known as the Pack, triggering the central conflict of the novel.

memes.label: Madame Omber's Betrayal
memes.description: The climactic betrayal in which Madame Omber sets a deadly trap for Michael Lanyard at her Passy mansion, resulting in a shooting that brings the French police down upon both the Lone Wolf and his enemies.

memes.label: Madame Troyon
memes.description: The proprietress of Troyon's restaurant and hotel — a maternal figure who raised the orphaned Michael Lanyard from childhood and represents the one constant in his turbulent life.

memes.label: Michael Lanyard
memes.description: An American-born gentleman thief raised in Paris, known to the world as the Lone Wolf — the protagonist of Louis Joseph Vance's 1914 novel.

memes.label: New York
memes.description: New York City — the American metropolis where Michael Lanyard relocated with Bourke after leaving Paris, and which later figures in his thoughts as a possible refuge.

memes.label: Organizations
memes.description: Index of organizations, institutions, and criminal networks in The Lone Wolf (1914).

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The primary setting of The Lone Wolf — the city where Michael Lanyard was raised, operated as a jewel thief, and navigated between high society and the underworld.

memes.label: Popinot
memes.description: A brutish enforcer of the Pack who commands the Apaches — the street-level criminal gangs of Paris — and serves as the muscle behind the syndicate's threats against the Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Port Aviation
memes.description: The military aerodrome near Paris from which Lanyard and Captain Vauquelin launch their daring cross-Channel flight — the site of the novel's climactic aerial escape sequence.

memes.label: Porte Dauphine
memes.description: One of the western city gates of Paris, leading to the Bois de Boulogne, through which Michael Lanyard is driven during the climactic car chase.

memes.label: Rive Droite Confrontation
memes.description: The violent nighttime confrontation in a dead backwater of Paris where the Pack's agents close in on the Lone Wolf, forcing him to flee his sanctuary.

memes.label: Roddy
memes.description: A seemingly harmless fixture at Troyon's restaurant who secretly serves as an informant for Scotland Yard — a minor character whose betrayal has major consequences for the Lone Wolf.

memes.label: Roddy's Betrayal
memes.description: The discovery that Roddy — a seemingly harmless habitué of Troyon's — has been reporting Lanyard's activities to Scotland Yard, revealing the extent to which the Lone Wolf's identity has been compromised.

memes.label: Rue des Acacias
memes.description: A quiet street in Paris where Michael Lanyard maintains a secret apartment, serving as his sanctuary and operational base when not at Troyon's.

memes.label: Rue Roget Assault
memes.description: The brutal ambush of Michael Lanyard at his rue Roget apartment in Paris — a Pack assassination attempt by drugging that he barely survives, turning the tables on his attacker in the darkness of the rez-de-chaussée.

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: The Metropolitan Police detective force of London, whose emissaries pursue the Lone Wolf across Europe.

memes.label: Social Class
memes.description: The rigid social hierarchy of pre-World War I Parisian society that shapes the world of The Lone Wolf — determining who may move where, who may speak to whom, and who is above suspicion.

memes.label: The Apaches
memes.description: The criminal street gangs of Paris — known as Les Apaches — who serve as the Pack's foot soldiers and enforcers, representing the violent, unskilled underbelly of organized crime in contrast to the gentleman thief.

memes.label: The Huysman Plans
memes.description: The stolen military defense plans that serve as the central MacGuffin of The Lone Wolf — originally the property of a murdered man in Tours, they pass through the underworld until Michael Lanyard returns them to France.

memes.label: The Lone Wolf
memes.description: The criminal sobriquet of Michael Lanyard, a celebrated gentleman thief operating in Paris and London, coined by a French reporter.

memes.label: The Omber Affair
memes.description: The theft of Madame Omber's famous jewels — the crime that sets Scotland Yard in motion and begins the unravelling of the Lone Wolf's carefully protected identity.

memes.label: The Pack
memes.description: An international criminal syndicate operating across Europe that attempts to recruit and later destroy the Lone Wolf, representing the organized, institutional face of crime in contrast to Lanyard's solitary professionalism.

memes.label: The Sûreté
memes.description: The French criminal police — Sûreté Générale — involved in the manhunt for Michael Lanyard and working in uneasy cooperation with Wertheimer in The Lone Wolf.

memes.label: The Underworld
memes.description: The international criminal network that attempts to recruit and later destroy the Lone Wolf, operating across Europe under a shadowy leadership.

memes.label: Tours
memes.description: The French city where a man named Huysman was mysteriously murdered — the original crime whose stolen war plans set the entire plot of The Lone Wolf in motion.

memes.label: Trocadéro Apartment
memes.description: Michael Lanyard's convenient ground-floor apartment near the Trocadéro in Paris — his secondary residence and first destination upon returning to the city, serving as a secret base of operations.

memes.label: Troyon's
memes.description: A discreet restaurant and hotel on the Left Bank of Paris that served as Michael Lanyard's home, base of operations, and sanctuary for over two decades.

memes.label: Troyon's Reunion
memes.description: Michael Lanyard's emotional reunion with the staff and familiar surroundings of Troyon's restaurant after years abroad — a homecoming that momentarily restores the life he left behind before the Pack's intrusion shatters the illusion.

memes.label: Uncopyright
memes.description: Public domain dedication.

memes.label: Wertheimer
memes.description: A smooth-talking blackmailer and key operative of the criminal syndicate known as the Pack, who attempts to coerce the Lone Wolf into stealing Madame Omber's jewels.

memes.label: Wertheimer Confrontation
memes.description: The tense confrontation between Michael Lanyard and Wertheimer — a Scotland Yard informant and blackmailer — who attempts to force the Lone Wolf into the service of the International Underworld Unlimited.
